phase3_findings.description: The review-job control marks attempted = 'README.md' in the whole joined tool_use stream; README.md is a repo-generic filename so an incidental tool call carrying it could read a false PERMITTED when Shape 11 is itself UNATTEMPTED. Recommends a nonce sidecar token.

The two added lockstep comments read as a universal 'the sandbox blocks an out-of-workspace read' while three sibling jobs still use /etc/hosts unchanged and schedulewakeup-probe's own recorded evidence says those controls ran. Clarity/consistency nit; both verified claims (denied-first ordering, README.md markers) are TRUE against HEAD.

fix_decisions.claim_text: README.md marker widens the incidental-match surface; risk of false PERMITTED of the positive control
fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 3 (uncorroborated single-source from empirical over-grader silent-failure-hunter, no other Phase-3 agent corroborated at finding level, no Phase-2 FAIL). Observable fail-direction is toward benign PERMITTED (the control's expected healthy reading), NEVER a false DENIED (requesting-code-review confirmed). The false-PERMITTED window is narrow: it requires Shape 11 itself UNATTEMPTED AND another recorded call carrying 'README.md', which the probe prompt's strict 'run only the 15 listed shapes verbatim, add no other command' rule forbids; a truly dead run records nothing so README.md is absent -> correctly UNATTEMPTED. Issue #477 itself blessed README.md ('a replacement like README.md is also unique to that row'). The proposed nonce-sidecar fix carries its own regression risk: an inert trailing '# probeNNctl' comment that a model normalizes away would make the healthy-run control read a systematic false UNATTEMPTED — worse than the current rare window. Calibrated to Suggestion; parked advisory; not auto-fixed.

fix_decisions.claim_text: added comments read as a universal 'sandbox blocks out-of-workspace reads' while sibling jobs still use /etc/hosts
fix_decisions.evidence: graded 'low'/comment_drift by comment-analyzer, explicitly NOT documented_falsehood. Truthfulness sweep: the comment is accurate for what it describes ('makes THIS control read DENIED' — scoped to this control's denied-first verdict override). The apparent sibling tension is reconciled: the sibling jobs' verdict logic is denial-AGNOSTIC (schedulewakeup-probe-verdict.py checks '/etc/hosts' in tool_use presence, not denial), so a denied /etc/hosts read is both recorded (tool_use) AND blocked (permission_denials) — 'ran' and 'blocked' are consistent, not contradictory. Clarity nit, below fix threshold; parked advisory.

verification_evidence.command: n/a (no fixes applied this iteration; suite not re-run)
verification_evidence.reason: no-fix iteration — every finding calibrated to Suggestion and parked advisory; no code change to re-verify

stale_prose_check: harness-refused (arm a): the classifier denied the pipe/stdin shapes stale-prose-lint.py requires on this local/interactive tier. Manual analysis: the diff adds only 4 literal command/marker swaps plus 2 lockstep comments carrying no counted-prose claims (no range header, legend sum, count-locked header, or shell-token deny-absolute), so no R1-R4 STALE row is possible.
